data.title: Eramet surges after buying remaining stake in Argentina lithium project
data.description:
Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) +8.2% in European trading Thursday after the French miner agreed to acquire full control of a lithium project in Argentina from Chinese partner Tsingshan Holding Group in a deal worth $699M.

Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) said it wanted to retake full ownership to fully control the development of the resource, which currently has capacity to produce 24K tons/year of lithium carbonate equivalent, enough for 600K electric vehicles annually, with potential long-term production of more than 75K tons/year lithium-carbonate equivalent.

The Centenario project, due to start production in the coming weeks, was attractive despite a drop in lithium prices, and full ownership would let Eramet (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY)decide how to pursue a planned second production facility, Chair and CEO Christel Bories said.

The French government, which is one of Eramet's (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) biggest shareholders, reacted positively to the deal, with Energy and Climate Minister Agnès Pannier-Runacher saying it "strengthens our critical raw materials sovereignty."

Tsingshan remains Eramet's (OTCPK:ERMAF) (OTCPK:ERMAY) partner in Indonesia, where they jointly operate a nickel mine.

data.title: Walgreens, Veeva Systems form partnership
data.description:
• Walgreens (NASDAQ:WBA) has struck a long-term partnership with Veeva Systems (NYSE:VEEV) to help life sciences companies improve patient outcomes.
  • The partnership enriches Veeva Data Cloud products and allows Walgreens (WBA) to better serve life sciences through access to Veeva Data Cloud and Veeva Clinical Platform.
  • "Partnering with Walgreens expands the breadth of data in Veeva Data Cloud, giving life sciences companies the high-quality data they need to better understand market trends and improve patient outcomes,” said Veeva CEO Peter Gassner.

data.title: Southwest reaches deal with Elliott Management, Q3 results beat expectations
data.description:
Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) has surrendered its early morning gains tied to Q3 results as investors react to the news that the carrier has reached a deal with Elliott Management that would see CEO Bob Jordan keep his job, but board chairman Gary Kelly “accelerate” his retirement. Elliott also secured the appointment of six new directors to Southwest’s board, effectively ending the risk of a proxy war.

Shares have slipped into negative territory after climbing as much as 6% in a knee-jerk reaction to its latest quarterly results.

As part of its agreement with Elliott, Kelly, along with the previously announced retirements of six other Southwest (LUV) directors, will go into effect November 1, 2024, after which Kelly will assume the title of Chairman Emeritus and a new Chairman will be appointed. The board will subsequently be reduced to 13 members as of the company’s 2025 annual shareholder meeting. The refreshed Finance Committee will be chaired by Gregg Saretsky, the former CEO of WestJet.

“We are pleased to reach a collaborative resolution with Elliott,” Kelly said, adding that, “I believe Southwest’s best days lie ahead under the vision and leadership of Bob Jordan and the oversight of this reconstituted Board.”

The news overshadowed otherwise encouraging results from the company, in which third quarter profit and revenue were both above Wall Street’s expectations even as profits were slashed by two-thirds.

Weighed down by high maintenance and labor costs that drove operating expenses up 12% and the adjusted cost per available seat mile (“CASM”) up 11%, the carrier’s profit fell to just $0.15 per share, although this was still better than expectations for a profit of just 4 cents per share. Passenger revenue per seat mile, or PRASM, increased by just 3.3% year-over-year and was down nearly 5% from the previous quarter. Total revenue increased, however, and beat Wall Street’s expectations by $80M.

The airline carried 0.5% more passengers in Q3 than a year ago and the load factor increased 50 basis points to 81.2%.

For Q4, accounting for cancelations tied to Hurricane Milton, Southwest (LUV) expects unit revenue to increase by 3.5% to 5.5% with capacity down ~4%. CASM excluding fuel, oil, special items, and profit-sharing will likely increase by 11% to 13%.

“Thus far in the quarter, travel demand remains healthy and bookings-to-date for the holiday season are strong, demonstrating the continued resilience of the leisure travel market. The guidance range represents another sequential year-over-year unit revenue improvement driven by the company's focus on tactical actions including network optimization and capacity moderation, marketing and distribution evolution, and continued efforts to advance revenue management techniques,” Southwest Airlines said in a statement.

Southwest (LUV) shares were down 1.2% ahead of Thursday’s regular open.

data.title: ABM enters into partnership with Mainspring Energy
data.description:
• ABM (NYSE:ABM) said it has entered into a strategic partnership with Mainspring Energy.
  • Under the partnership, the firm will deploy Mainspring's Linear Generator technology to boost electric vehicle charging solutions for fleet, commercial and industrial clients.
  • The two companies will also provide engineering, procurement, and construction services, and essential maintenance support.

data.title: Honeywell's stock slides on lower earnings guidance for year
data.description:
Honeywell International’s (NASDAQ:HON) stock fell 2.9% in premarket trading Thursday after the aerospace and technology missed quarterly sales estimates and revised its guidance downward.

Net income was $1.41 billion, or $2.16 a share, in the third quarter, compared with $1.51 billion, or $2.27 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, the company posted earnings of $2.58 a share, beating the average estimate of $2.50 a share among Wall Street analysts.

Sales rose 6% to $9.73 billion from $9.21 billion, missing the consensus of $9.9 billion.

The company cut its full-year guidance to a range of $38.6 billion to $38.8 billion, down from $39.1 billion to $39.7 billion previously.

Management also expects adjusted earnings of $10.15 to $10.25 a share, compared with prior guidance of $10.05 to $10.25 a share.

“Honeywell (HON) executed through a challenging environment in the third quarter, delivering segment margin and adjusted earnings per share above the high end of our guidance,” Chief Executive Vimal Kapur said in prepared remarks. The company “continues to see healthy order rates and sequential growth in our backlog, even excluding the impact of acquisitions closed in the quarter, giving us confidence in our ability to achieve our long-term targets.”

Strength in defense and space, commercial aviation and building solutions drove sales for the period. Operating income fell 4% and its margin contracted by 180 basis points, mostly because of an impairment from classifying the company’s personal protective equipment business as assets held for sale.

Realigning Businesses

Honeywell (HON) is divesting from the PPE business and selling its Advance Materials unit into an independent company as part of an effort to focus on key high-margin businesses.

The company this year announced four deals this year with the goal of higher growth in three key areas such as automation, aviation and clean energy.

In July, it announced the acquisition of Air Products’ liquefied-natural-gas-processing technology and equipment for $1.81 billion.

A month earlier, Honeywell (HON) said it was acquiring CAES Systems from private-equity firm Advent International for about $2 billion. That month, Honeywell (HON) closed on its $4.95 billion acquisition of Carrier Global (CARR) global access solutions business.

In March, the company said it would acquire Civitanavi Systems, an Italian aircraft-components manufacturer and developer.

data.title: Google's partnership with Anthropic formally probed by UK
data.description:
Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) partnership with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic is being formally probed by the U.K.'s antitrust regulator, the agency said on Thursday.

Shares of the tech giant rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

The Competition and Markets Authority said it is considering whether the partnership between the Sundar Pichai-led Google and Anthropic will impact competition. The CMA said it has set a deadline of December 19 to decide whether to launch into a Phase 2 investigation.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Google has invested a reported $2B into Anthropic and has deals with the OpenAI competitor to run its models on Google Cloud. Anthropic also has a deal with Google to use its newest artificial intelligence-focused processors, known as TPU v5e.

The CMA first announced in July that the deal between Google and Anthropic was under consideration for investigation. The agency is also looking into Amazon's (AMZN) deal with Anthropic.

In May, the CMA said a similar deal between Microsoft (MSFT) and French AI startup Mistral AI did not qualify for investigation.

data.title: Carpenter Technology Non-GAAP EPS of $1.73 beats by $0.14, revenue of $717.6M misses by $25.35M
data.description:
• Carpenter Technology press release (NYSE:CRS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.73 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $717.6M (+10.1% Y/Y) misses by $25.35M.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Outlook

    • Expect operating income to be at the high end of the previously provided range of $460 million to $500 million
    • Project $250 million to $300 million in adjusted free cash flow, which represents approximately 85 percent conversion rate
    • For the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, anticipate between $116 million and $123 million in operating income

data.title: InMode granted injunction against online sellers for Morpheus8 devices
data.description:
• InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) has been granted a preliminary injunction by the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California in a lawsuit filed against sellers identified to be the source of unlawful counterfeit sales of Morpheus8 radiofrequency devices and cartridge accessories.
  • On Sept. 3, 2024, the Honorable Michael Fitzgerald of the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California issued the order granting InMode's motion for a preliminary injunction.
  • InMode's large-scale action against these illegitimate sellers also warranted a Temporary Restraining Order, which was granted due to strong supporting evidence that it is within the public interest to protect InMode's trademarks and prevent the sale of counterfeit products.
  • "We will take necessary action with online platforms and sellers participating in the irresponsible use, sale or distribution of our brand and counterfeit products associated with it." said Moshe Mizrahy, CEO.
